message:
fix(db/sanitize): strip FTS5 metacharacters from sanitize_fts_query and reject all-punctuation inputs, plus property tests for MATCH and LIKE pipelines

sanitize_fts_query() was the guard in front of FTS5 MATCH interpolation.
FTS5 is currently decommissioned in this crate, but the sanitizer is the
correct place to harden because it's also reused upstream as input to
the LIKE fallback pipeline, and because any future re-enablement must
not have to re-audit the escaping.

- sanitize_fts_query():
  - Drop '{', '}', '^', '[', ']', '~', and '\\' from the output. These
    are the FTS5 characters that alter MATCH semantics (column filter
    braces, initial-token caret, bracket column syntax, NEAR tilde)
    plus raw backslash which has no FTS5 meaning and should never
    appear in a sanitized query token.
  - Collapse any resulting runs of spaces ("  " -> " ") so the strip
    doesn't leave sparse gaps.
  - Reject outputs that are empty or contain no alphanumeric
    characters after stripping — callers expect at least one token to
    match on, and all-punctuation inputs were previously surfacing as
    spaces-only strings.

- Two new proptest cases (inside the existing sanitize_fts_query module):
  - prop_fts_sanitize_no_match_operators: over arbitrary strings, any
    Some output must not contain /*, */, {, }, ^, [, ], ~, or \. One
    assertion per operator class so failures point directly at which
    operator leaked.
  - prop_like_pipeline_safe: over arbitrary strings up to 500 chars,
    extract_like_terms(... 10) → like_escape() → wrap in %...% must
    produce a pattern where the only unescaped % / _ wildcards are
    the outer wrapping ones. Iterates character-by-character honoring
    \X escape sequences. Also verifies per-term length ≥ 2 and that
    AND/OR/NOT/NEAR stopwords are never returned as extractable terms.
